identity.name: IoTeX
identity.one_liner: EVM-compatible L1 blockchain optimised for DePIN and real-world AI, providing identity, verification, and compute infrastructure for IoT devices.

founding_team.name: Raullen Chai
founding_team.background: PhD Electrical/Computer Engineering, University of Waterloo (cryptography). Former Senior Software Engineer/Tech Lead at Google, Lead Crypto R&D at Uber.

founding_team.name: Qevan Guo
founding_team.background: PhD AI/Computer Vision, NUS. Former product lead at Facebook (Ads, Pages, Offers).

founding_team.name: Jing Sun
founding_team.background: Founding partner Sparkland Capital. 40+ investments in security, blockchain, IoT.

founding_team.name: Xinxin Fan
founding_team.background: PhD University of Waterloo. Former Senior Research Engineer at Bosch R&D.

technical.architecture_summary: EVM-compatible L1 with Roll-DPoS consensus (DPoS + PBFT + VRFs). 36 consensus delegates, 24 randomly selected each epoch. 2.5s block time, 1-block finality, 1000 TPS. Core stack: IoTeX L1, ioID (device identity via DIDs/VCs as NFTs), W3bstream (ZK-verified device data), QuickSilver (AI agent framework), DIMs (modular DePIN tooling).
technical.consensus_mechanism: Roll-DPoS — 36 community-elected consensus delegates, 24 randomly selected per epoch via VRF/BLS. Block time 2.5s, instant finality.
technical.what_is_onchain: Transaction settlement, staking/delegation, ioID device registration (NFTs), governance, smart contracts (Solidity), token transfers.
technical.what_is_offchain: W3bstream computation and ZK proof generation, QuickSilver AI processing, IoT device data collection.

audits.result_summary: No vulnerabilities found. Formal verification completed.

token_utility: Burn-Drop mechanism tied to device onboarding
tokenomics.burn_mechanism: Multiple: (1) Burn-Drop — 1B IOTX allocated, ~400M burned so far (4% of supply). (2) IoTeX 2.0 EIP-1559-style gas burning. (3) ioID registration burns.
tokenomics.emission_model: Disinflationary with IoTeX 2.0 introducing new inflationary staking offset by burns.

usage_metrics.usage_notes: 3,010 ioID device owners. 218 ecosystem projects including 51 DePIN. DePINscan tracks 280+ DePIN projects globally.

methods.description: Stake IOTX to delegates. 70+ delegates with varying reward policies. Auto-stake 91+ days for Burn-Drop airdrops.
Run an IoTeX delegate node. Top 36 by stake produce blocks.
Build DePIN apps using DIMs (ioID, W3bstream, QuickSilver). EVM-compatible Solidity. IoT hardware SDKs.
Vote on IIPs via Snapshot. Any IOTX holder can vote.

freedom_score.freedom_summary: IoTeX scores solidly in the partially decentralised range. Strongest in data sovereignty (ioID and W3bstream provide genuine device-level data control with ZK verification) and open source transparency. Main centralisation concerns are the small delegate set (36 consensus validators), foundation-controlled implementation, and low governance voter turnout.

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: Own L1 with Roll-DPoS. 36 consensus delegates, 60+ total, 24 randomly selected per epoch via VRF. Permissionless node operation. Small delegate set and foundation's role in upgrades are centralisation vectors. Mainnet live since April 2019.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: Functional IIP governance process via Snapshot. IIP-42 passed unanimously but only 60 voters with ~31M IOTX. Foundation and MachineFi Lab retain implementation control. Low voter turnout concerning.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: Team 15% + Foundation 25% = 40% insider allocation. ICO was public at $0.0083. 94.4% now circulating. Burn-Drop burned ~400M IOTX (4%). Initial allocation was heavy but now mostly vested.

censorship_resistance.evidence: Permissionless EVM-compatible chain. No KYC for transacting. Roll-DPoS with 36 rotating validators provides reasonable resistance. Smaller validator set than Ethereum but functional.

data_sovereignty.evidence: Core design principle. ioID gives devices self-sovereign identity via DIDs/VCs as NFTs. W3bstream ZK proofs verify device data without exposing raw data. Device keys stored on-device. Among the strongest data sovereignty architectures in DePIN.

open_source_transparency.evidence: Apache-2.0 licence. 173 public repos. 3,598 commits, 1,584 stars. Founder Raullen Chai personally committing code March 2026. CertiK audit completed. IIP process public. Some DIM modules less fully documented.

token_utility.evidence: IOTX serves as gas, staking/delegation, governance, and ioID device registration (burned). Multiple utility vectors but gas usage minimal ($2.58M TVL). Only 3,010 ioID device owners. Utility is real but volume is low.

value_accrual.evidence: Burn-Drop removed ~400M IOTX (4%), gas burning planned for IoTeX 2.0, ioID registration burns. With $2.58M TVL actual value captured is minimal. No clear fee revenue flowing to holders beyond staking inflation.

supply_dynamics.evidence: 94.4% of 10B max supply circulating. Minimal future dilution. Burn-Drop burned ~400M tokens. IoTeX 2.0 introduces inflationary staking counterbalanced by burns — net effect uncertain.

revenue_sustainability.evidence: Gas fees from 250K daily txns but fees extremely low. $2.58M TVL. No Token Terminal or DeFiLlama revenue data. Protocol revenue heavily dependent on staking inflation, not organic revenue. $70M funding provides runway but protocol revenue is negligible.

liquidity_access.evidence: Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, Robinhood, KuCoin, Gate.io, MEXC. 40+ platforms. $4.66M daily volume. Good exchange coverage for $52M market cap. PayPal/Venmo fiat on-ramps.
